definition: A practitioner who has weakened key fetters and is said to return to this world at most once.
notes: The project keeps once-returner because it is established, concise, and doctrinally specific. The longer phrase one who returns once is acceptable when a sentence needs to breathe more naturally in English. The term belongs inside the graded liberation sequence and should be read as an intermediate noble stage, not as a stand-alone honorific.

context_rules.context: In doctrinal classifications of awakening stages
In explanatory prose with fuller sentence rhythm
When used in the graded sequence between stream-enterer and non-returner
context_rules.notes: Keep the progression of attainment explicit.

example_phrases.notes: Useful compound linking stage language back to magga terminology.
